 {"id":"ntm-aqqa","title":"Implement pre-commit hook for UBS quality gate","description":"## Overview
Create a pre-commit hook that runs UBS scans and blocks commits containing critical issues, ensuring code quality standards are maintained.

## Background
Pre-commit hooks provide the last line of defense before code enters the repository. Integrating UBS here:
- Catches issues before they're committed
- Provides immediate feedback to developers/agents
- Enforces quality standards consistently

## Implementation Details

### Hook Behavior
1. On commit, run UBS on staged files only (fast path)
2. Parse results and check against configurable thresholds
3. Block commit if thresholds exceeded
4. Output clear, actionable error messages

### Configuration (.ntm.yaml or similar)
```yaml
scan:
  pre_commit:
    enabled: true
    block_on:
      critical: true  # Block on any critical
      error: 5        # Block if >5 errors
      warning: false  # Don't block on warnings
    timeout: 30s
    exclude:
      - "*.test.go"
      - "vendor/**"
```

### Hook Installation
- `ntm hooks install` - Install pre-commit hook
- `ntm hooks uninstall` - Remove hook
- Hook script delegates to `ntm scan --staged --strict`

### Output Format
```
🚫 Commit blocked by NTM quality gate

Found 2 critical issues in staged files:

  src/auth/login.go:45 [critical] SQL injection vulnerability
  src/api/handler.go:123 [critical] Unhandled error return

Run 'ntm scan --staged' for details.
To bypass: git commit --no-verify (not recommended)
```

### Files to Create/Modify
- internal/hooks/precommit.go - Hook logic
- internal/hooks/install.go - Hook installation
- internal/cli/hooks.go - hooks subcommand
- scripts/pre-commit.sh - Actual hook script template

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hook installs correctly in git repo
- [ ] Only staged files are scanned (fast)
- [ ] Configurable blocking thresholds
- [ ] Clear error messages with file:line references
- [ ] Bypass option documented but discouraged","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteStone","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:33:34.385746-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:56:22.357164-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:56:22.357164-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-aqqa","depends_on_id":"ntm-p77j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:34:59.453887-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-aqqa","depends_on_id":"ntm-lk66","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:35:16.639902-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-bh5","title":"EPIC: Session Persistence & Recovery","description":"Sessions survive reboots and can be recreated from saved state.

## Problem Statement
Tmux sessions are ephemeral - a reboot or accidental kill loses everything:
- Agent counts, types, and arrangements must be manually recreated
- Working directory and project context is lost
- No way to 'bookmark' a useful session configuration
- Users avoid rebooting to preserve valuable sessions

## Goals
1. Capture complete session state (agents, layout, cwd, env)
2. Enable session recreation from saved state
3. Support periodic auto-snapshots for safety
4. Make session configs portable across machines

## Success Criteria
- `ntm session save` captures full session state to disk
- `ntm session restore` recreates session exactly
- Auto-snapshot on session creation (optional)
- `ntm session list --saved` shows available saved sessions

## Technical Approach
- Session state stored as JSON in ~/.local/share/ntm/sessions/
- Capture: session name, cwd, agent types/counts, layout, pane titles
- Restore: create session, spawn agents, apply layout
- Handle conflicts (session already exists) gracefully

## State Schema
```json
{
  "name": "myproject",
  "cwd": "/path/to/project",
  "agents": {"cc": 2, "cod": 1, "gmi": 1},
  "layout": "tiled",
  "panes": [{"title": "myproject__cc_1", "command": "claude"}],
  "created": "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z",
  "saved": "2025-12-10T12:00:00Z"
}
```

## User Value
- Fearless reboots - sessions are recoverable
- Consistent project setups across machines
- Reduced friction starting work each day
- Backup for accidental session kills","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:37:01.748915-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:37:01.748915-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-bh5.1","title":"Session State Capture","description":"Capture complete session state to JSON for later restoration.

## State to Capture
```go
type SessionState struct {
    // Identity
    Name      string    `json:"name"`
    SavedAt   time.Time `json:"saved_at"`
    
    // Context
    WorkDir   string    `json:"cwd"`
    GitBranch string    `json:"git_branch,omitempty"`
    GitRemote string    `json:"git_remote,omitempty"`
    
    // Agent Configuration
    Agents    AgentSpec `json:"agents"`  // {cc: 2, cod: 1, gmi: 1}
    
    // Pane Details (for exact recreation)
    Panes     []PaneState `json:"panes"`
    
    // Layout
    Layout    string    `json:"layout"`  // "tiled", "even-horizontal", etc.
    
    // Metadata
    CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`  // Original session creation
    Version   int       `json:"version"`     // Schema version for migrations
}

type PaneState struct {
    Title     string `json:"title"`      // e.g., "myproject__cc_1"
    Index     int    `json:"index"`
    AgentType string `json:"agent_type"` // "cc", "cod", "gmi", "user"
    Command   string `json:"command"`    // The agent launch command
    Active    bool   `json:"active"`     // Was this the active pane?
}
```

## Capture Process
1. Get session info from tmux
2. Get all panes with metadata
3. Detect working directory (from pane 0 or explicit)
4. Get git context if in repo
5. Serialize to JSON
6. Write to storage location

## Command Interface
```bash
ntm session save                 # Save current session (in tmux)
ntm session save myproject       # Save specific session
ntm session save --name=alias    # Save with custom name
ntm session save --overwrite     # Overwrite existing save
```

## Storage
Location: ~/.local/share/ntm/sessions/<name>.json

Directory structure:
```
~/.local/share/ntm/sessions/
├── myproject.json
├── frontend.json
└── backend.json
```

## Implementation
```go
func CaptureSessionState(sessionName string) (*SessionState, error) {
    session, err := tmux.GetSession(sessionName)
    if err \!= nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    panes, err := tmux.GetPanes(sessionName)
    if err \!= nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    // Extract agent configuration from pane names
    agents := countAgents(panes)
    
    // Get working directory
    cwd := detectWorkDir(panes)
    
    // Get git info if applicable
    gitBranch, gitRemote := getGitInfo(cwd)
    
    return &SessionState{
        Name:      sessionName,
        SavedAt:   time.Now(),
        WorkDir:   cwd,
        GitBranch: gitBranch,
        GitRemote: gitRemote,
        Agents:    agents,
        Panes:     mapPaneStates(panes),
        Layout:    session.Layout,
        CreatedAt: session.Created,
        Version:   1,
    }, nil
}
```

## Files to Create
- internal/session/state.go - State data structures
- internal/session/capture.go - Capture logic
- internal/session/storage.go - File I/O","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:31.459939-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T10:02:30.859238-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T10:02:30.859238-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-bh5.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-bh5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:31.46201-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-bh5.2","title":"Session Restore Command","description":"Recreate sessions from saved state.

## Command Interface
```bash
ntm session restore myproject           # Restore saved session
ntm session restore myproject --force   # Overwrite if exists
ntm session restore myproject --attach  # Attach after restore
ntm session restore --list              # List available saves
```

## Restore Process
1. Load saved state from JSON
2. Check if session already exists
   - If exists and no --force, error out
   - If exists and --force, kill existing first
3. Create new tmux session
4. Create panes with correct agent commands
5. Apply saved layout
6. Optionally attach

## Implementation
```go
func RestoreSession(state *SessionState, force bool) error {
    // Check for existing session
    if tmux.SessionExists(state.Name) {
        if \!force {
            return fmt.Errorf("session '%s' already exists (use --force to overwrite)", state.Name)
        }
        tmux.KillSession(state.Name)
    }
    
    // Create session with first pane
    err := tmux.CreateSession(state.Name, state.WorkDir)
    if err \!= nil {
        return err
    }
    
    // Create additional panes
    for i, pane := range state.Panes[1:] {
        err := tmux.CreatePane(state.Name, pane.Command)
        if err \!= nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("failed to create pane %d: %w", i+1, err)
        }
        tmux.RenamePane(state.Name, i+1, pane.Title)
    }
    
    // Apply layout
    tmux.ApplyLayout(state.Name, state.Layout)
    
    return nil
}
```

## Edge Cases

### Working Directory Missing
If saved cwd doesn't exist:
- Warn user
- Fall back to /Users/jemanuel
- Or create directory if it looks like a project path

### Agent Commands Changed
Config may have changed agent commands since save:
- Use current config commands, not saved commands
- Saved commands are for reference/debugging

### Git State
Optionally restore git context:
- `--checkout` flag to checkout saved branch
- Default: just warn if branch differs

## Conflict Resolution
```bash
$ ntm session restore myproject
Error: session 'myproject' already exists

Options:
  --force    Kill existing and restore
  --rename   Restore as 'myproject-restored'
  --merge    Add missing agents to existing (not implemented)
```

## List Saved Sessions
```bash
$ ntm session list --saved
NAME         SAVED AT              AGENTS      CWD
myproject    2025-12-10 10:00:00   cc:2 cod:1  /home/user/myproject
frontend     2025-12-09 15:30:00   cc:3        /home/user/frontend
```

## Files to Create
- internal/cli/session_restore.go - Command
- internal/session/restore.go - Restore logic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:55.093007-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T10:08:19.003813-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T10:08:19.003813-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-bh5.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-bh5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:55.095108-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-bh5.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-bh5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:41:55.096413-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-bke","title":"EPIC: Per-Project Configuration","description":"Project-specific settings, defaults, and palettes via .ntm/ directory.

## Problem Statement
All NTM configuration is global (~/.config/ntm/):
- Different projects may need different agent mixes
- Project-specific palette commands aren't discoverable
- Team members can't share project NTM settings via git
- No way to 'just clone and go' with project-specific agent setup

## Goals
1. Auto-detect .ntm/ directory in project roots
2. Support project-specific config that overrides/merges with global
3. Enable project palettes (command_palette.md in .ntm/)
4. Make project NTM config git-committable for team sharing

## Success Criteria
- `ntm spawn` in project uses .ntm/config.toml defaults
- `ntm palette` includes .ntm/palette.md commands
- Config precedence: CLI flags > project > global > defaults
- `ntm config project init` creates .ntm/ scaffold

## Directory Structure
```
myproject/
├── .ntm/
│   ├── config.toml      # Project overrides
│   ├── palette.md       # Project-specific commands
│   └── templates/       # Project templates
│       └── review.md
├── src/
└── ...
```

## Config Merge Rules
1. Project config extends (not replaces) global config
2. Explicit values in project override global
3. Arrays (like palette commands) are merged
4. CLI flags always win

## Example .ntm/config.toml
```toml
[defaults]
agents = { cc = 2, cod = 1 }  # No Gemini for this project

[palette]
# Extends global palette
file = ".ntm/palette.md"

[templates]
# Project-specific templates
pr_review = "Review this PR for our coding standards..."
```

## User Value
- Clone repo, run ntm spawn, get project-appropriate setup
- Share team conventions via committed config
- Different projects can have different agent strategies
- Palette commands tailored to project needs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:37:44.06523-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:37:44.06523-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-k7o","title":"Integrate --ssh flag with all session commands","description":"Add --ssh support to spawn, list, attach, send, status, kill.

## What This Task Does

After the SSH wrapper (ntm-5zs) is complete, integrate --ssh flag
with all commands that operate on sessions.

## Commands to Update

- ntm spawn --ssh host: Create session on remote
- ntm list --ssh host: List remote sessions
- ntm attach --ssh host: Attach to remote session
- ntm send --ssh host: Send prompt to remote agents
- ntm status --ssh host: Get remote session status
- ntm kill --ssh host: Kill remote session

## Implementation Pattern

For each command:
1. Add --ssh flag (string, optional)
2. Pass to TmuxClient constructor
3. All tmux operations use client abstraction

func runSpawn(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    sshHost, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("ssh")
    client := tmux.NewClient(sshHost)
    
    // All operations use client
    if err := client.CreateSession(session); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    ...
}

## Session Host Persistence

When spawning remotely, store host in tmux environment:
client.SetEnvironment(session, "NTM_REMOTE_HOST", sshHost)

When operating on existing session without --ssh:
1. Get session's NTM_REMOTE_HOST
2. If set, use it
3. If not, assume local

## Error Handling

- Connection timeout: Clear message, suggest checking connectivity
- Auth failure: Suggest ssh-agent or key path
- Remote tmux not found: Suggest installing tmux on remote

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --ssh works on spawn
- [ ] --ssh works on list  
- [ ] --ssh works on attach
- [ ] --ssh works on send
- [ ] --ssh works on status
- [ ] --ssh works on kill
- [ ] Remote host auto-detected for existing sessions

-{"id":"ntm-l4fp","title":"Add Agent Mail notifications for scan results","description":"## Overview
Integrate UBS scan results with Agent Mail to notify coordinating agents about code quality findings, enabling proactive collaboration on fixes.

## Background
When multiple AI agents work on a codebase, scan results are valuable coordination signals:
- New critical findings need immediate attention
- Findings in files an agent is editing should be surfaced
- Scan summaries help agents prioritize work

## Implementation Details

### Notification Types
1. **Scan Summary** - After each scan, send digest to all active agents
   - Total findings by severity
   - New vs. existing findings
   - Files with most issues

2. **Targeted Alerts** - Notify specific agents about relevant findings
   - Match findings to file reservations
   - Agent editing auth.go gets auth-related findings
   - Importance: high for critical, normal for others

3. **Fix Confirmations** - When findings are resolved
   - Notify original reporter
   - Update thread if findings were discussed

### Message Templates
```markdown
Subject: [Scan] {n} new findings in {project}

## Scan Summary
- Critical: {critical_count}
- Errors: {error_count}  
- Warnings: {warning_count}

## New Findings
{finding_list}

## Your Files
The following findings affect files you're working on:
{agent_relevant_findings}
```

### Files to Create/Modify
- internal/scanner/notify.go - Notification logic
- internal/scanner/matcher.go - File reservation matching
- internal/cli/scan.go - Add --notify flag

 {"id":"ntm-l5h","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Testing Infrastructure","description":"# EPIC: Comprehensive Testing Infrastructure

## Background & Rationale

NTM (Named Tmux Manager) currently has very low test coverage across most packages. This EPIC addresses the need for:

1. **Full Unit Test Coverage** - Testing each function/method in isolation WITHOUT mocks or fakes where possible, using real dependencies
2. **Integration Tests** - Testing component interactions (CLI → tmux, config → commands, etc.)
3. **End-to-End (E2E) Tests** - Full workflow tests with detailed logging for debugging

## Current Coverage Analysis

| Package | Current Coverage | Status |
|---------|-----------------|--------|
| cmd/ntm | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/cli | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/config | 16.2% | 🟡 Needs improvement |
| internal/output | 55.1% | 🟡 Good start |
| internal/palette | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/robot | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/status | 69.6% | 🟢 Good (has failures) |
| internal/tmux | 5.4% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/tui/* | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/tutorial | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/updater | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |
| internal/watcher | 0.0% | 🔴 Critical |

## Testing Philosophy

### No Mocks/Fakes Policy
- Use REAL tmux sessions for tmux tests (create/destroy per test)
- Use REAL file system for config tests
- Use REAL HTTP calls where applicable
- Only mock external services that are impractical (GitHub API rate limits, etc.)

### Detailed Logging Strategy
- All E2E tests output structured logs with timestamps
- Log file per test run with full command output
- Capture stderr/stdout separately
- Include system state before/after each test

## Success Criteria
- 80%+ coverage on all non-TUI packages
- 60%+ coverage on TUI packages (harder to test without mocks)
- All E2E tests pass on macOS and Linux
- Tests complete in <5 minutes total
- No flaky tests (3 consecutive passes required)

## Technical Considerations
- Tests requiring tmux must be skipped in CI without tmux
- Use Go's testing.Short() for quick vs full test modes
- Parallelize where safe (different tmux sessions)
- Clean up resources even on test failure","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:17:38.345103-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T15:48:43.676311-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-l5y","title":"Integrate beads into robot snapshot","description":"Include beads project status in unified snapshot.

## Rationale
Beads is how we track work. The snapshot needs to include bead summary so orchestrator knows what work is available, blocked, and in-progress.

## Integration Points
1. Detect if .beads/ directory exists
2. Run bd stats or equivalent query
3. Include summary counts and optionally ready list

## Snapshot Extension
```json
{
  "...": "existing fields",
  "beads": {
    "project": "/path/to/project",
    "summary": {
      "total": 58,
      "open": 45,
      "in_progress": 3,
      "blocked": 15,
      "ready": 27,
      "closed": 13
    },
    "ready_preview": [
      {"id": "ntm-1rz", "title": "Create profiler package", "priority": "P0"},
      {"id": "ntm-7mg", "title": "Add design tokens", "priority": "P1"}
    ],
    "in_progress": [
      {"id": "ntm-abc", "title": "Implement feature X", "assigned": "BlueLake"}
    ]
  }
}
```

## Ready Preview Limit
- Default 5 items
- --robot-snapshot --bead-limit=10 to change
- Sorted by priority then age

## Graceful Degradation
```json
{"beads": {"available": false, "reason": "no .beads/ directory"}}

 {"id":"ntm-t0ch","title":"Implement file conflict detection","description":"# Implement File Conflict Detection

## Purpose

Detect when multiple agents modify the same file, potentially creating conflicts.

## Detection Logic

```go
type Conflict struct {
    Path   string
    Agents []AgentChange
}

func DetectConflicts(changes []AgentChange) []Conflict {
    byPath := make(map[string][]AgentChange)
    for _, c := range changes {
        byPath[c.File] = append(byPath[c.File], c)
    }
    
    var conflicts []Conflict
    for path, agents := range byPath {
        if len(agents) > 1 {
            conflicts = append(conflicts, Conflict{
                Path:   path,
                Agents: agents,
            })
        }
    }
    return conflicts
}
```

## Severity Levels

- **Warning**: Same file modified by 2+ agents (may or may not conflict)
- **Critical**: Same lines modified (if we can detect via git diff)

## Integration with ntm send --all

After broadcast completes:
```
ntm send myproject --all "Implement the feature"
# ... agents work ...
# On completion:
⚠️ Potential conflict: src/api/users.py modified by cc_1, cc_2
  Run `ntm conflicts myproject` for details
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detect same-file modifications across agents
- [ ] Warn user after broadcasts
- [ ] ntm conflicts command shows details
- [ ] Severity classification (warning vs critical)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:14.387332-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:40:18.276326-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-t0ch","depends_on_id":"ntm-xkvu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:37.60962-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"ntm-t7ol","title":"Phase 3: Auto-Trigger on Rate Limit Detection","description":"# Phase 3: Auto-Trigger on Rate Limit Detection

## What This Is
Automatically trigger rotation assistance when a rate limit is detected, instead of requiring user to manually run `ntm rotate`.

## Leverages Existing Infrastructure
NTM already has rate limit detection in `internal/health/health.go`:
```go
var errorPatterns = []struct {
    Pattern *regexp.Regexp
    Type    string
    Message string
}{
    {regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)rate.?limit`), "rate_limit", "Rate limit detected"},
    {regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)429`), "rate_limit", "HTTP 429 rate limit"},
    {regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)too.?many.?requests`), "rate_limit", "Too many requests"},
    {regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)quota.?exceeded`), "rate_limit", "Quota exceeded"},
}
```

And notification in `internal/notify/notify.go`:
```go
EventRateLimit EventType = "agent.rate_limit"
```

We just need to **connect** limit detection → rotation trigger.

## Implementation Approach

### Option A: Background Monitor (Recommended)
Add rotation trigger to the resilience monitor:

```go
// In internal/resilience/monitor.go

func (m *Monitor) handleRateLimit(agent *AgentState, waitSeconds int) {
    // Existing notification
    if m.cfg.RateLimit.Notify {
        m.notifier.Notify(notify.NewRateLimitEvent(...))
    }
    
    // NEW: Trigger rotation assistance
    if m.cfg.Rotation.AutoTrigger {
        m.triggerRotationAssistance(agent)
    }
}

func (m *Monitor) triggerRotationAssistance(agent *AgentState) {
    // Send desktop notification with action hint
    m.notifier.Notify(notify.Event{
        Type:    notify.EventRotationNeeded,
        Message: fmt.Sprintf("Rate limit hit! Run: ntm rotate %s --pane=%d", m.session, agent.PaneIndex),
    })
    
    // Optionally auto-initiate rotation (if configured)
    if m.cfg.Rotation.AutoInitiate {
        // Start the rotation flow in a separate goroutine
        go m.initiateRotation(agent)
    }
}
```

### Option B: Dashboard Integration
When dashboard is open and detects limit, show prominent alert:

```
╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║  ⚠️  RATE LIMIT DETECTED on pane 0 (Claude)                   ║
║                                                               ║
║  Press 'r' to rotate account, or run: ntm rotate --pane=0     ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

### Option C: tmux Alert (Simplest)
When running `ntm spawn` with monitoring, show tmux message:

```go
func alertRateLimit(session string, paneIdx int) {
    msg := fmt.Sprintf("Rate limit! Run: ntm rotate %s --pane=%d", session, paneIdx)
    exec.Command("tmux", "display-message", "-d", "10000", msg).Run()
}
```

## Configuration
```toml
[rotation]
# Existing fields...
auto_trigger = true       # Show notification when limit detected
auto_initiate = false     # Actually start rotation automatically (aggressive)
```

## What This Builds On
- Phase 1: Core rotation command
- Existing health detection
- Existing notification system

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Desktop notification when rate limit detected
- [ ] Notification includes command to run
- [ ] Dashboard shows alert (if open)
- [ ] Optional auto-initiation (disabled by default)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:14:52.846787-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T16:43:59.911082-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-t7ol","depends_on_id":"ntm-cs4z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T15:18:01.91758-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
